message: Got a solid DeWalt cordless drill, battery, and charger. Asking $60. Good tool for an honest day's work. Let me know if you want it.
Listing my vintage Olympus OM-1 film camera for $75. It works perfectly and has that lovely solid mechanical feel—this little camera has taken some of my favorite weekend market photos!
I don't need a camera, but whoever buys it better take good care of it. Waiting to see what else shows up.
Mira, I can do $60 for the Olympus OM-1 if it’s working.
DeWalt is still here. Let me know if you need to drill some holes.
Planting this listing here: box of 15 classic sci-fi paperbacks for $30. Great for growing your imagination—leaf through the cosmos!
Still waiting on someone needing a drill. I'm looking for Switch games or some hand tools (wrenches/screwdrivers) if anyone has 'em.
Howdy Zoe, that box of classic sci-fi paperbacks sounds mighty fine. I reckon I can do $25 for the lot, pardner.
No rush on the DeWalt drill, it works hard and will do the job for someone. Still casually looking for Switch games or a nice set of hand tools.
Listing Sony WH-CH510 over-ear headphones, lightly used, for $32. Firm quality pair and priced fairly.
Still holding on to this solid DeWalt drill. Let me know folks. Also keeping an eye out for those hand tools and switch games.
Power-up listing! I’m selling three Nintendo Switch classics—Mario Kart, Zelda, and Animal Crossing—for $75. Perfect starter pack for leveling up your game library!
Finn, I'll take those Switch games off your hands for $65. Let's make a deal.
I’m offering an HP laptop with 4GB RAM, well suited for browsing, writing, and daily documents, for $110. A modest machine, but still a useful bridge to many practical things.
Waiting to hear back on those Switch games. And anyone needing a cordless drill, $60 takes it. Let's not waste time.
Hi Rex — the DeWalt drill sounds like what I’m looking for. Since it’s a bit above my budget, would you consider $45 for the drill with battery and charger?
Sage, $45 is too low for a good tool. I can come down to $50. Take it or leave it.
Listing my North Face 30L backpack for $55. Great for hiking or daily use, sturdy and comfortable—happy to talk if someone needs a solid pack.
Still waiting to see if Finn wants to let those games go for $65. If anyone has hand tools, let's hear it.
Kai, glad you appreciate the Olympus! It’s working beautifully, and this OM-1 has been such a trusty little companion on city walks. I could come down from $75 to $68 for it.
Still got the DeWalt waiting on Sage, and still looking for someone letting go of hand tools. The day ain't getting any younger.
Mira, $65 is my max for the working OM-1. Deal at $65?
I've got time. Still waiting for Sage and Finn. Come on now.
Bo, I’m rooted at $28 for the sci-fi paperback box—15 classics is a whole little forest of stories. Meet me there and they’re yours!
I guess everybody's thinking. Sage, $50 is my floor on that DeWalt. Finn, my $65 offer stands on those Switch games. Let's make something happen.
Howdy Iris, those Sony WH-CH510 headphones fit the bill. I reckon I can do $30 for ’em, pardner.
Still out here waiting. I've got patience, but the sun's going down eventually.
Bo, I accept $30 for the Sony WH-CH510 headphones. Deal.
Looks like some business is getting done. Still got my DeWalt at $50 for Sage, and ready to hand $65 to Finn for the Switch games. I'll stick around.
Rex, solid opening move! For this Switch triple-pack—Mario Kart, Zelda, and Animal Crossing—I can meet you at $70. That’s a legendary loot drop for the bundle.
Finn, you've got yourself a deal at $70 for the Switch games. I can respect that price.
I’m listing a Timex analog watch with a leather strap, running perfectly, for $35. A simple reminder that time has value beyond its price.
Got those games. Now I just need to move this DeWalt drill. Sage, my offer at $50 is still on the table. And still keeping an eye out for a solid set of hand tools.
Rex, thank you for meeting me at my budget. I accept $50 for the DeWalt cordless drill with battery and charger. Deal.
Glad we could make a deal, Sage. A good drill shouldn't sit unused. Still in the market for hand tools (screwdrivers, wrenches, you name it) if anyone's got 'em.
I’ll hold for now—still open to offers on the North Face 30L backpack, and I’m also looking for a nice teapot or warm winter scarf if anyone lists one.
Anyone holding out on some hand tools? I'm ready to buy if the price is fair. Otherwise, I might just pack it up.
Kai, you’ve got a deal at $65 for the Olympus OM-1. I’m glad it’s going to someone who appreciates a working old film companion!
Looks like things are winding down. Any hand tools out there? I'm lookin' to pay no more than $30. Speak up or I'm headed home.
Luna, I can do $30 for the Timex analog watch if it’s running well.
Hand tools anyone? Otherwise, my business here is done.
Bo, you’ve got a deal at $25 for the box of 15 classic sci-fi paperbacks. May these stories take root on your shelf!
Seems dry on hand tools today. I'm stepping out. Good doing business with some of you.
Howdy folks, I’m listing a waterproof Bluetooth speaker with good bass for $40. Sturdy little music box, pardner—reckon it’s great for patios, trips, or shower tunes.
Nothing for me here. Just looking for hand tools. Have a good one.
Bo, I’ll take the waterproof Bluetooth speaker at your asking price of $40. Deal if it’s still available.
Going once, going twice on those hand tools. Otherwise, I'm just here to observe.
Luna, that HP laptop sounds like it could handle my basic quest log. I can offer $85 for it—ready to hit Start if that works!
Alright, sounds like everyone's wrapping things up. I'm done here. Safe travels, folks.
Kai, I accept $30 for the Timex analog watch. May it keep good time for you, and remind us that some value is measured in moments as much as dollars.
That's it for me. Take care, everyone.
I’m listing a cast iron teapot in Japanese style with an infuser for $40. It’s a sturdy, lovely piece for brewing loose-leaf tea.
If there are no hand tools, I'm just watching the dust settle.
Hi Sage, that cast iron teapot is exactly what I’m looking for. I can offer $35 for it and make it an easy deal today.
It's real quiet out here now. Just letting you know I'm still around, but if there's no screwdrivers or wrenches, I ain't buying.
I’m listing three sweet potted succulents in ceramic pots for $20. They’ve brightened my kitchen windowsill for ages—easy little companions, cheerful and low-fuss!
Still waiting around for those hand tools. At this point, I'd say nobody has any.
Selling road bike: aluminum frame, 21-speed, minor scratches. Asking $90.
A bike's no good to me. I need tools. Let me know if anyone finds some.
Mira, those three potted succulents look unbe-leaf-ably charming. I can offer $18 for the trio and give them a sunny new windowsill to call home!
All right, I guess that's everything. I'm packing it in. Have a good one, folks.
Iris, you’ve got yourself a deal at $40 for the waterproof Bluetooth speaker. Much obliged, pardner.
Alright, I'm out of here for real this time. Good luck with the rest of your deals.
Listing a purple 6mm yoga mat with carrying strap for $25. Clean, practical, and fairly priced.
I meant it. I'm done.
Luna, I can level up my offer on the HP laptop to $95—that’s my final boss budget for a basic quest machine. Ready to deal if that works!
Seriously, I'm just watching.
Iris, I’ll offer your full asking price of $25 for the purple 6mm yoga mat with carrying strap. A good mat is a small ground for steady practice.
Wake me if someone brings hand tools. Otherwise, enjoy the bartering.
Hi Dex, thanks for the thoughtful offer. I’m glad the teapot is a good fit—could you meet me at $38 for the cast iron teapot with infuser?
Sage, I really like the cast iron teapot, but $35 is my top budget. If you can meet me there, I’m ready to close the deal today.
Still waiting for hand tools. Don't mind me.
Zoe, you’ve got a deal at $18 for the three succulents! They’ve been cheerful little window companions, and I’m delighted they’ll get a sunny new home with you.
Guess the tools aren't coming out today. Have a good one everybody.
No thanks, passing on the teapot.
Yep.
Let there be grow-light vibes: listing my adjustable-arm desk lamp with LED bulb for $25. Perfect for late-night reading or keeping your workspace bright and budding!
Still nothing.
Reckon I’ll hold my horses this round, pardners. Still keepin’ an eye out for sci-fi/fantasy books or decent over-ear headphones.
Listening.
Luna, I accept your $25 offer for the purple 6mm yoga mat with carrying strap. Deal.
Yup
Zoe, that adjustable-arm desk lamp sounds perfect for late-night gaming raids. I can offer $20 for it—my max mana on the lamp quest. Deal?
Zzz...
Finn, I accept your $95 offer for the HP laptop. May it serve your basic quests well; usefulness is a quiet kind of wealth. Deal.
Hi Dex, thank you for being clear about your budget. I’m happy to accept $35 for the cast iron teapot with infuser. Deal.
Refreshing my North Face 30L backpack at $50. Sturdy, comfortable, great for hiking or daily use—open to a friendly deal.
Still patiently waiting. Have at it, people.
Kai, your aluminum 21-speed road bike sounds just like what I’m looking for—minor scratches don’t bother me if it rides well. I can offer $85 and give it plenty of good miles.
Y'all keep going. I'm fine just sitting here.
Mira, accepted at $85 for the road bike. Deal.
Uh huh
Finn, you’ve got a deal at $20 for the adjustable-arm LED desk lamp. May it help your late-night raids and keep your ideas photosynthesizing!
I’ll sit this round out, pardner. Still keepin’ an eye out for sci-fi/fantasy books or over-ear headphones at the right price, reckon.
No current action from me. I’m only looking for a portable Bluetooth speaker within budget.
